module.name: ast-guards
module.description: AST node type guards and related type aliases used across multiple rule implementations.

scenarios.name: should return true for a BinaryExpression node
scenarios.given: an AST node with type BinaryExpression
scenarios.then: returns true and narrows the type to TSESTree.BinaryExpression

scenarios.name: should return false for a non-BinaryExpression node
scenarios.given: an AST node with a different type
scenarios.then: returns false because the node is not a BinaryExpression

scenarios.name: should return false for null or undefined
scenarios.given: null or undefined
scenarios.then: returns false without throwing

scenarios.name: should return true for a BlockStatement node
scenarios.given: an AST node with type BlockStatement
scenarios.then: returns true and narrows the type to TSESTree.BlockStatement

scenarios.name: should return false for a non-BlockStatement node
scenarios.given: an AST node with a different type
scenarios.then: returns false because the node is not a BlockStatement

scenarios.name: should return true for a CallExpression node
scenarios.given: an AST node with type CallExpression
scenarios.then: returns true and narrows the type to TSESTree.CallExpression

scenarios.name: should return false for null or undefined
scenarios.given: null or undefined
scenarios.then: returns false without throwing

scenarios.name: should return true for a FunctionDeclaration node
scenarios.given: an AST node with type FunctionDeclaration
scenarios.then: returns true and narrows the type to TSESTree.FunctionDeclaration

scenarios.name: should return false for FunctionExpression or ArrowFunctionExpression
scenarios.given: an AST node with type FunctionExpression
scenarios.then: returns false because function expressions are not declarations

scenarios.name: should return true for an Identifier node
scenarios.given: an AST node with type Identifier
scenarios.then: returns true and narrows the type to TSESTree.Identifier

scenarios.name: should return false for null or undefined
scenarios.given: null or undefined
scenarios.then: returns false without throwing

scenarios.name: should return true for a MemberExpression node
scenarios.given: an AST node with type MemberExpression
scenarios.then: returns true and narrows the type to TSESTree.MemberExpression

scenarios.name: should return false for null or undefined
scenarios.given: null or undefined
scenarios.then: returns false without throwing

scenarios.name: should return true for a MethodDefinition node
scenarios.given: an AST node with type MethodDefinition
scenarios.then: returns true and narrows the type to TSESTree.MethodDefinition

scenarios.name: should return false for null or undefined
scenarios.given: null or undefined
scenarios.then: returns false without throwing

scenarios.name: should return true for an object with a string type property
scenarios.given: an object with a string-valued type property
scenarios.then: returns true and narrows the type to TSESTree.Node

scenarios.name: should return false for null
scenarios.given: the value null
scenarios.then: returns false without throwing

scenarios.name: should return false for an object with a non-string type property
scenarios.given: an object where type is a number
scenarios.then: returns false

scenarios.name: should return false for a primitive value
scenarios.given: a string or number value
scenarios.then: returns false

scenarios.name: should return true for a SwitchCase node
scenarios.given: an AST node with type SwitchCase
scenarios.then: returns true and narrows the type to TSESTree.SwitchCase

scenarios.name: should return false for null or undefined
scenarios.given: null or undefined
scenarios.then: returns false without throwing

scenarios.name: should return true for a .test.ts filename
scenarios.given: a filename ending in .test.ts
scenarios.then: returns true because the path matches a recognized test-file suffix

scenarios.name: should return true for a .spec.ts filename
scenarios.given: a filename ending in .spec.ts
scenarios.then: returns true because the path matches a recognized test-file suffix

scenarios.name: should return true for a file inside __tests__ directory
scenarios.given: a filename whose path contains /__tests__/
scenarios.then: returns true because __tests__ paths are treated as test files

scenarios.name: should return false for a regular source file
scenarios.given: a filename with no test indicator
scenarios.then: returns false because the path does not match any recognized test-file pattern

scenarios.name: should normalise Windows backslash separators
scenarios.given: a Windows-style path using backslashes containing __tests__
scenarios.then: returns true after path normalisation

scenarios.name: should return true for a TSEnumMember node
scenarios.given: an AST node with type TSEnumMember
scenarios.then: returns true and narrows the type to TSESTree.TSEnumMember

scenarios.name: should return false for null or undefined
scenarios.given: null or undefined
scenarios.then: returns false without throwing

scenarios.name: should return true for a UnaryExpression node
scenarios.given: an AST node with type UnaryExpression
scenarios.then: returns true and narrows the type to TSESTree.UnaryExpression

scenarios.name: should return false for null or undefined
scenarios.given: null or undefined
scenarios.then: returns false without throwing

scenarios.name: should return true for a VariableDeclarator node
scenarios.given: an AST node with type VariableDeclarator
scenarios.then: returns true and narrows the type to TSESTree.VariableDeclarator

scenarios.name: should return false for null or undefined
scenarios.given: null or undefined
scenarios.then: returns false without throwing
